op.text: Applied to six-month ceramics residency at coastal craft school; application submitted 2025-12-19 at 11:52pm with portfolio of fall 2025 pots. Residency includes studio access, stipend, cohort. Recommendation letter from Saturday intensive teacher. Described as 'long shot.' Has not told work yet; would require leave or further reduced hours if accepted. (as of 2025-12-20)

On 2025-12-20, describing the residency application, Dana said: 'A year ago the idea of stepping back from my career for half a year to throw pots would have read to me as a breakdown. Now it reads as the sanest thing I've considered in ages.' Dana said: 'I think I finally understand the difference between what I do and who I am. Design — product work — is what I do. I'm good at it, it pays the mortgage, I don't hate it. But it is not who I am, and I spent fifteen years with those two things fused so tightly I couldn't see the seam. Who I am is closer to — someone who makes things with her hands and looks after the places and people her mother loved. That's not a hobby that got out of control. That's the actual center, and the job is the thing arranged around it now, not the other way around.' Dana said: 'It took me all year and a dead mother and a sprained ankle and a boat to get there, but yeah. Design is what I do. It's not who I am. I could not have told you that in February.' (2025-12-20)
